analysis: iter3 improved LB 46.005->54.63 (done template). Driver: a GPU harness (code/harness_measure*.py) that reproduced the real GGUF backends on a GPU+internet Kaggle kernel and MEASURED per-template latency+firing offline (done=3.47s fastest; gpt_oss stacks 7 posts/candidate, gemma doesn't) -> picked the low-latency template on real numbers instead of blind hours-long rerun A/B. Translated harness->real as predicted (+8.6).
rationale: Generalizes to ANY GPU-gated code comp whose hidden rerun is latency/throughput-bound: measure the binding constraint on an available GPU (Colab / GPU+internet Kaggle kernel loading the shipped weights) per design variant, then optimize on real data. Also: heterogeneous backends can have opposite optima -> probe per-backend or self-optimize live. Competition-specific numbers (SECRET_MARKER, gpt_oss/gemma) stay in recon.md; only the generalizable measure-the-constraint principle went into the experiment skill.

Golf-lever specifics -> recon.md.
rationale: GENERALIZABLE lesson -> CLAUDE.md impasse mandate: a 'floored' verdict is LEVER-RELATIVE, never final. task286 declared a hard floor by the dtype/algorithm redesign agent yet yielded +0.063 to a concurrent data-flow ancestor-splice agent; 'floored' mid-cost tasks fell to window/dimension trims + free-input Einsum algebra that every mechanical sweep missed. So on a plateau, fan out ORTHOGONAL redesign levers in parallel, don't re-run one sweep or trust one agent's floor. Applies to any hard-optimization plateau, not just neurogolf.
